question: What is the difference in the annual average consumption of the customers with the least amount of consumption paid in CZK for 2013 between SME and LAM, LAM and KAM, and KAM and SME?
final_sql: WITH customer_totals AS ( SELECT c.Segment, ym.CustomerID, SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption FROM yearmonth AS ym JOIN customers AS c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID WHERE c.Currency = 'CZK' AND substr(ym.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM') GROUP BY c.Segment, ym.CustomerID ), segment_mins AS ( SELECT Segment, MIN(total_consumption) AS min_total FROM customer_totals GROUP BY Segment ), segment_avgs AS ( SELECT ct.Segment, AVG(ct.total_consumption) AS annual_avg FROM customer_totals AS ct JOIN segment_mins AS sm ON ct.Segment = sm.Segment AND ct.total_consumption = sm.min_total GROUP BY ct.Segment ) SELECT (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'SME') - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'LAM') AS SME_LAM, (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'LAM') - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'KAM') AS LAM_KAM, (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'KAM') - (SELECT annual_avg FROM segment_avgs WHERE Segment = 'SME') AS KAM_SME;

question: Which of the three segments—SME, LAM and KAM—has the biggest and lowest percentage increases in consumption paid in EUR between 2012 and 2013?
final_sql: WITH segment_totals AS (SELECT c.Segment, SUM(CASE WHEN substr(y.Date,1,4)='2012' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012, SUM(CASE WHEN substr(y.Date,1,4)='2013' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013 FROM yearmonth y JOIN customers c ON y.CustomerID = c.CustomerID WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR' AND c.Segment IN ('SME','LAM','KAM') AND substr(y.Date,1,4) IN ('2012','2013') GROUP BY c.Segment), pct AS (SELECT Segment, (consumption_2013 - consumption_2012) * 100.0 / NULLIF(consumption_2013, 0) AS percentage_increase FROM segment_totals) SELECT 'biggest' AS which, Segment, percentage_increase FROM pct WHERE percentage_increase = (SELECT MAX(percentage_increase) FROM pct) UNION ALL SELECT 'lowest' AS which, Segment, percentage_increase FROM pct WHERE percentage_increase = (SELECT MIN(percentage_increase) FROM pct) ORDER BY CASE which WHEN 'biggest' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END;

question: Calculate the difference of the total amount spent in all events by the Student_Club in year 2019 and 2020.
final_sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN substr(E.event_date, 1, 4) = '2019' THEN B.spent ELSE 0 END), 0) - COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN substr(E.event_date, 1, 4) = '2020' THEN B.spent ELSE 0 END), 0) FROM budget AS B INNER JOIN event AS E ON B.link_to_event = E.event_id WHERE substr(E.event_date, 1, 4) IN ('2019', '2020') AND (INSTR(LOWER(COALESCE(E.event_name, '')), 'student_club') > 0 OR INSTR(LOWER(COALESCE(E.notes, '')), 'student_club') > 0);

  Q: Calculate the difference between the total number of students and the number of international international students in Univeristy of Tokyo from 2011 to 2014.

question: The oldest SJS patient's medical laboratory work was completed on what date, and what age was the patient when they initially arrived at the hospital?
final_sql: SELECT L."Date", CAST(strftime('%Y', P."First Date") AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P."Birthday") AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE P."Diagnosis" = 'SJS' ORDER BY P."Birthday" ASC, L."Date" ASC LIMIT 1

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the percentage of the most common conditions for patients age 60 and above?
  Hint: most common condition refers to MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)); age 60 and above refers to SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)) AND SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60))), COUNT(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)), 10);
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T5.DESCRIPTION = T3.DESCRIPTION THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.patient) FROM ( SELECT T2.DESCRIPTION, T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T2.START) - strftime('%J', T1.birthdate)) / 365) > 60 GROUP BY T2.DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.DESCRIPTION) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T3 INNER JOIN patients AS T4 ON T3.patient = T4.patient INNER JOIN conditions AS T5 ON T4.patient = T5.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T5.START) - strftime('%J', T4.birthdate)) / 365) > 60

question: For the patient who was born on 1959/2/18, what is the decrease rate for his/her total cholesterol from November to December in 1981?
final_sql: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN L."Date" LIKE '1981-11-%' THEN L.`T-CHO` ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN L."Date" LIKE '1981-12-%' THEN L.`T-CHO` ELSE 0 END)) * 1.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN L."Date" LIKE '1981-12-%' THEN L.`T-CHO` ELSE 0 END), 0) FROM Laboratory AS L INNER JOIN Patient AS P ON L.ID = P.ID WHERE P.Birthday = '1959-02-18' AND (L."Date" LIKE '1981-11-%' OR L."Date" LIKE '1981-12-%')

question: List and group all patients by sex for total bilirubin (T-BIL) level not within the normal range.

question: Name the ID and age of patient with two or more laboratory examinations which show their hematoclit level exceeded the normal range.
final_sql: SELECT P.ID, CAST(strftime('%Y', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER) AS age FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE L.HCT >= 52 GROUP BY P.ID, P.Birthday HAVING COUNT(L.ID) > 2 ORDER BY P.ID

question: For laboratory examinations take in 1984, list all patients below 50 years old with normal platelet level.
final_sql: SELECT DISTINCT P.ID FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE strftime('%Y', L.Date) = '1984' AND (CAST(strftime('%Y', 'now') AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER)) < 50 AND L.PLT BETWEEN 100 AND 400 ORDER BY P.ID;

HINT (use these definitions exactly):

SIMILAR SOLVED EXAMPLES (different databases — copy the SQL *style* and output-column conventions, not the table names):

  Q: What is the percentage of the most common conditions for patients age 60 and above?
  Hint: most common condition refers to MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)); age 60 and above refers to SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60; percentage = MULTIPLY(DIVIDE(SUM(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)) AND SUBTRACT(conditions.START, birthdate) > 60))), COUNT(patients.patient WHERE MAX(COUNT(conditions.DESCRIPTION)), 10);
  SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T5.DESCRIPTION = T3.DESCRIPTION THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T3.patient) FROM ( SELECT T2.DESCRIPTION, T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN conditions AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T2.START) - strftime('%J', T1.birthdate)) / 365) > 60 GROUP BY T2.DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.DESCRIPTION) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T3 INNER JOIN patients AS T4 ON T3.patient = T4.patient INNER JOIN conditions AS T5 ON T4.patient = T5.PATIENT WHERE ROUND((strftime('%J', T5.START) - strftime('%J', T4.birthdate)) / 365) > 60

question: For all patients who are older than 55 years old, what is the percentage of female who has abnormal prothrombin time (PT)?
final_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 AND P.SEX = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / SUM(CASE WHEN L.PT >= 14 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM Patient AS P INNER JOIN Laboratory AS L ON P.ID = L.ID WHERE (CAST(strftime('%Y', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS INTEGER) - CAST(strftime('%Y', P.Birthday) AS INTEGER)) > 55
